_publications._publicationTitle: Synergistic Suppression of NF1 Malignant Peripheral Nerve Sheath Tumor Cell Growth in Culture and Orthotopic Xenografts by Combinational Treatment with Statin and Prodrug Farnesyltransferase Inhibitor PAMAM G4 Dendrimers.
_publications._context: The MPNST cell line ST88-14 was obtained from T. Glover (University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI, USA). | Human MPNST cell line ST88-14, S462 and a patient-derived xenoline RHT92 were kindly provided by Melissa L. Fishel | The ST88-14 (RRID: CVCL_8916) and M724 (MPNST724, RRID: CVCL_AU20) human MPNST cell lines were gifted by Dr. Jonathan A. Fletcher | NF90-8 and ST88-14 MPNST cell lines were generously donated by T. Glover (University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI) | PDX cells and the MPNST cell line, ST88-14 (gift from Dr. Nancy Ratner, PhD, University of Cincinnati) | malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumour (MPNST) cell lines ST88-14, S462 and NF90-8 | T265-2c cells, ST88-14 cells and 90-8 cells, the human NF1-associated MPNST lines used in this study | Two million ST88‐14 (RRID: CVCL_8916) cells mixed 1 : 1 with Matrigel | ST8814 was derived from a patient with NF1-associated MPNST of the thigh | Immortalized human Schwann cell and MPNST cell lines (S462 [ 34 ], S462-TY [ 35 ], ST8814 [ 36 ], T265 [ 37 ], and STS-26T [ 38 ]) | human MPNST cell lines (ST88-14, STS26T, S462, S462-TY, and T265) | We maintained MPNST cell lines ST88-14, STS26T, S520, S462, 88-3, 90-8 and YST1 | Several cell lines derived from MPNSTs were used: S462 and ST88-14 (provided by Dr Nancy Ratner) | MPNST cell lines STS26T, ST8814, ST88-3 S462, T265p21, S520, 90–8, and YST1...All cell lines were from NF1 patients except YST-1 and STS26T | ST-8814 and S462 cells were obtained from Dr. Andrew Tee (Cardiff University) | Patient-derived neurofibroma (NF95.11b, NF95.6) or MPNST (SNF02.2, SNF94.3, SNF96.2, ST88-14) cell lines | The MPNST cell lines STS26T, T265, and ST8814 have been reported before and were gifted by Dr. Nancy Ratner | Immortalized human Schwann cell and MPNST cell lines (S462 ( 45 ), S462-TY ( 35 ), ST8814 ( 46 ), T265 ( 47 ), and STS-26T ( 36 )) | ST88-14 is an established NF1-deficient MPNST cell line | STS26T, ST8814 and NF90.8 were kindly provided by Dr. Gregory Riggins at Johns Hopkins University
